infobox.value: This article about a radio station in Coahuila is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=XHWQ-FM&action=edit).

is a radio station on [103.1 FM](/wiki/103.1_FM "103.1 FM") in [Monclova](/wiki/Monclova "Monclova"), [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila"), [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico"). It is known as La Súper Estación.

1330 received its concession on August 6, 1958. It was owned by José Boone Menchaca and broadcast for many years with 1,000 watts day and 250 watts night. In the 1990s, it raised its daytime power to 4,000 watts. In October 2011, XEWQ was cleared for AM-FM migration.

[Instituto Federal de Telecomunicaciones](/wiki/Federal_Telecommunications_Institute "Federal Telecommunications Institute"). [Infraestructura de Estaciones de Radio FM](http://www.ift.org.mx/sites/default/files/contenidogeneral/industria/memoriafm160518.pdf). Last modified 2018-05-16. Retrieved 2015-06-10. Technical information from the [IFT Coverage Viewer](http://mapasradiodifusion.ift.org.mx/CPCREL-web/).

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Radio station in Monclova, Coahuila, Mexico

XHWQ-FM

**XHWQ-FM** is a radio station on [103.1 FM](/wiki/103.1_FM "103.1 FM") in [Monclova](/wiki/Monclova "Monclova"), [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila"), [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico"). It is known as La Súper Estación.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=XHWQ-FM&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

**XEWQ-AM** 1330 received its concession on August 6, 1958. It was owned by José Boone Menchaca and broadcast for many years with 1,000 watts day and 250 watts night. In the 1990s, it raised its daytime power to 4,000 watts. In October 2011, XEWQ was cleared for AM-FM migration.
